title: Djavad Salehi-Isfahani analysis of Iran's SCI Household Expenditure & Income Survey (HEIS), 1402/1403 (2023/24, 2024/25) rounds
source_org: Djavad Salehi-Isfahani (Professor of Economics, Virginia Tech; blog 'Tyranny of numbers'), analyzing primary microdata from the Statistical Centre of Iran (SCI)

files.name: post_2025-09-20_modest-gains-2024-2025.html
files.post_title: Household Survey Data Reveal Modest Gains in Iran's Living Standards for 2024/2025

files.name: post_2024-10-22_rising-living-standards.html
files.post_title: New Data Reveal Rising Living Standards in Iran

time_coverage: SH1402-1403 (2023/24, 2024/25) HEIS survey rounds analyzed in these two posts; the author's underlying dataset/figures reference back to 1984 but only the 2023-2025 figures were extracted this pass
units: mixed -- percent growth rates, percent poverty rate, Gini index (0-1), rials/USD-PPP for cash transfers; see data.csv for per-row units
license_terms: Blog content, publicly readable; treated as an independent academic secondary source analyzing SCI's own primary HEIS microdata -- author is a named, credentialed economist (Virginia Tech), not an anonymous or advocacy outlet, and is NOT on this project's exclusion list (MEK/NCRI, IRGC-propaganda, Tudeh, Fadaian)
notes: Found via WebSearch while hunting for SCI HEIS household-expenditure coverage beyond this project's existing 2001-2020 extraction (data/raw/sci-amar/household-expenditure-detail-2001-2020/), which was the most recent HEIS-derived data already in the project. amar.org.ir itself is geo-blocked from this network for both direct and Wayback-Machine access on every attempt logged in this repo (see download log) -- this author's published analysis of SCI's own primary microdata is the best available route to SH1402-1403 (2023-2025) household-economic figures found this pass. IMPORTANT CAVEAT, spelled out explicitly per this project's 'flag rather than silently treat as primary' convention: these are the AUTHOR's derived statistics (regional per-capita-expenditure growth rates, a poverty rate computed against the author's own chosen poverty line -- Iran has no official poverty line -- and a Gini coefficient), not verbatim SCI-published headline figures; do not present as if SCI itself published these exact numbers. Spot-checked 2 figures (poverty rate 17.5%->16.6%, cash transfer 9,000,000 rials/month) directly against the raw saved HTML text before transcribing (both confirmed present verbatim) rather than trusting a first-pass extraction blindly.
